creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
> It wasn't any mistake

Of course, it was -- as proven by the fact, that nothing was done to the port in question. The fact, that ownCloud requires php74 was OVERLOOKED -- which overlooking was a mistake. A mistake made worse by the overly fast removal of the php74.

> announced in advance by bofh@.

The announcement clearly didn't reach sunpoet@, did it? Not in time, anyway. Probably, because the time was TOO SHORT.

> If the port committers act the way you demand, not purging some ports on time

I do want purging to happen ON TIME, indeed, that's my whole point...

> giving the illusion of backporting security patches to the software which was EOL upstream

Contrary to any FUD-spreading attempts, security flaws do not appear automatically the day a software is declared EOL. In the absence of actual (not hypothetical) remotely-exploitable security problems, there is no grounds for removing a port.

Moreover, even if such actual security problems were known, the proper reaction would've been to list the port in vuln.xml -- and, maybe, even mark it BROKEN. But not remove the entire family "just in case"...

It is these practices, that violate the users' trust and should be avoided.

Look at lang/gcc48, for example. It is marked DEPRECATED, but the expiration is set to May of 2024 -- that's the timescale I'd expect.

devel/log4j is also still with us, despite being abolished upstream -- abolished not 1 month, but 10+ years ago. We keep it, despite dangers^, because applications keep using it -- and that's, how things should be.

And that's, how things were, when I was evaluating FreeBSD... :)

^ https://security.snyk.io/package/maven/log4j:log4j/1.2.17
